question.text: Which of the following was NOT a problem of the earlier settlement system on Indian stock exchanges before the depository system was introduced?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The earlier system was plagued by bad deliveries, mutilation of share certificates, slow transfer of securities, theft, forgery, and other irregularities. Electronic transfer failures were not an issue since the system was paper-based. **The Trap:** 'Bad deliveries' were one of the most common problems — a significant proportion of transactions were recorded as bad deliveries due to faulty compliance of paper work. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation) covers the evolution from physical to electronic settlement. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The old system had two problem areas: (1) physical movement of securities caused delays, and (2) transfer of ownership required physical endorsement on certificates — both were slow, costly, and error-prone.
